title: M/M/1 Single-Server Queue
des_qualification_note: All required criteria satisfied BY FORMAL DEFINITION — M/M/1 is unique among DES models in that every criterion is part of the model's definition rather than an implementation property. Discrete entities (customers); discrete events (arrival, service start, service complete, departure); explicit simulated clock. Both typical criteria also by definition: resource contention at the single server; exponential inter-arrival and service time distributions make this the archetype of a stochastic queueing model.
summary_of_content_provenance: Foundational/pedagogical walkthrough with zero fetch friction. Three first-party direct sources, all HTML, all fetched successfully: Wikipedia M/M/1 queue article (First-party x Direct x Adjacent Method — uses closed-form queueing theory rather than simulation, but its formulas are the gold standard for validating M/M/1 simulations); Simio textbook Chapter 2 on queueing theory basics (First-party x Direct x DES — frames queueing theory in the simulation context); SimPy Bank tutorial (First-party x Direct x DES — official SimPy documentation demonstrating M/M/1 as a classic SimPy example). HTML-based foundational content has a structurally friendlier source ecosystem than applied research: no PDFs, no paywalls, no 403s. First walkthrough to exercise the proposal workflow — two domains (Queueing Theory, Pedagogical Models) are tagged as pending proposals for editorial review.
intended_audience_note: Foundational building block expected to be cross-referenced by nearly every queueing-based entry in SimVault. Serves as a pedagogical entry point and simulation validation benchmark rather than a decision-support model.
